user_prompt: the project is currently configured for WAV recording (no MP3 compression) and mic AGC is off.
I want a test of all mic gains.
Initialize the microSD card, the RTC, the `ExtCodec`. Use as much of the existing mechanisms as possible.
The earbud with an inline mic will be plugged in, this is the only thing I need to test.
Start an audio file recording. Start from the lowest mic gain that the SGTL5000 allows

record 0.5 second of actual silence, then 4 seconds of live audio from the mic, then 0.5 second of actual silence, then switch to the next possible gain setting. The I2C scenanigans should be masked within the silence
The file is closed and the test is over when we've tried all possible gain settings.
agent_reply: It now initializes RTC/Clock, microSD, shared I2S, `ExtCodec`, and `AudioManager`, requires the inline earbud mic, forces the SGTL5000 dedicated mic route, starts a `G-...wav` recording, then sweeps `AudioControlSGTL5000::micGain()` from `0` to `63`.

Serial reports init state, gain writes, phase progress, file bytes, FIFO fill, and final stats. I also bypassed `MicGainManager` in this test so software AGC/gating does not hide the hardware gain differences.
